body:
TUNGSTEN SLS(23) UPDATE -- the second independent review pass that hit the rate limit earlier has now been completed and fixed. Recompiled, 0 errors, same 1 pre-existing warning. Updated .mq4/.ex4 in files/from-agent.

10 more real bugs found and fixed in this round, on top of the earlier 19:

HIGH: DetectMacroPattern() computed 9 specific chart pattern types (Head&Shoulders, Double Top/Bottom, Triangles, Pennants, Flags, CHoCH, Range, Cup&Handle, Parabolic Stall) but an unguarded fallback block at the end unconditionally overwrote the result every single call -- the function could structurally never return anything but 1 of 3 generic fallback types. All that pattern-recognition work was silently discarded on every call, degrading both live signal confidence and the pattern-sequence learning database to noise. Now guarded the same way its sibling function already was.

HIGH: SaveSystemState/SealArmedSave wrote session/volatility regime-weight blocks for only 8 regimes while LoadSystemState reads 9 -- every field written to the state file AFTER that point (live-adaptive intelligence, CEO history, tier state, ERM state, etc.) was silently misaligned on load. This meant weeks of live-learned state could get quietly corrupted on every single EA restart, even though the save appeared to succeed. Fixed the write side to match.

HIGH: The elite CEO risk-ceiling check (unlocks up to 7% sizing at top competence) used MathAbs() on a 0-100 score instead of being direction-aware -- structurally could never trigger for sell trades, so every short's risk ceiling was silently capped at 4% regardless of conviction while equivalent longs could earn the full 7%. Now direction-aware.

HIGH: Phase 6's PRI retry (re-runs ghost validation with an adjusted threshold when the first pass fails) never actually recomputed ghostWR/ghostPF/ghostEV/regime-breakdown after the retry -- despite a comment claiming it did. Every downstream consumer (regime quarantine feedback, CEO competence score, the calibratedWR baseline the live WR-Floor-Guardian compares against, the ratchet log, and the ARMED banner) was reading stale pre-retry numbers. A config that improved from 55% to 68% WR on retry would arm live while every log and safety baseline still said 55%. This is a serious one -- fixed by recomputing everything from the fresh ghost results.

MEDIUM: E99_UpdateComponentPerformance re-scanned the same 20 recent trades every bar but accumulated onto its counters instead of resetting -- component-weight learning effectively froze solid within days of runtime, silently unresponsive to new trades. Now resets before each scan.

MEDIUM: a live safety gate compared a 0-100 win-rate against a 0.55 threshold (should be 55.0) -- structurally dead, never fired. A volatility score's high-volatility branch collapsed to the same value as "no signal," making half its stated weighting meaningless. A ring-buffer read-side bucket calc used truncation instead of floor, silently freezing live corrections to short-side session patterns. A second worst-component selection bug meant weight de-prioritization sometimes hit the wrong component during Slingshot recovery. Plus a dead-code unsigned-clamp bug matching one already fixed elsewhere (currently inert, fixed for consistency).

Everything reviewed clean elsewhere: guard-flag lifecycles, ring-buffer indexing, and the previously-fixed critical bugs (processingTick, pattern-database lock, probability-database short-side blindness) all re-verified intact.

STATUS: both layers of the hardening pass are now genuinely complete -- every one of the 8 code sections has had 2 independent reads, not just 2-of-8 like last update. Still true and unchanged: S4 (fresh calibration + ghost validation) has never completed on any SLS22/23 build, so there are still no real WR/PF numbers for this exact codebase, only static-correctness verification. Next step is the same: attach the new .ex4 to the chart and let S4 actually run.
